subject: Sync docker image versions from manifest
commit_message:
Sync docker image versions from manifest

Sync docker image versions from docker manifest commit
7682431276881e9bd27d6b15223ea9a9c2358c44.

Change-Id: Ifb494457d93b2ccec26b50a252fe34a6bcc0d133

Signed-off-by: Gary Wu <gary.i.wu@huawei.com>

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

We should discuss this - as currently patches are coming in one at a time for TSC-92 - each team should be putting these in and verifying them themselves - they require a deploy and healthcheck

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

Teams probably weren't expecting to have to individually submit the image changes in OOM directly since this sync method was the process used for the original Casablanca release.  I'm inclined to suggest that let's keep the same process for Casablanca MR, and formally announce and change the process for Dublin.

Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

talked to James Macnider - issue is 2 parts - I always had an issue with the fact that the manifest is only casablanca not 3.0.0-ONAP - so our pomba images went into 3.0.0-ONAP but they should not have gone into 3.0.1 - commenting on Gary's jira - james had a good point that the MR should be 3.1.0 not 3.0.1 that we should send out as well.   If we are going to use the manifest as truth then we will need versions for 3.0.1-ONAP as intent before cutting the tag - as just having a casablanca manifest is ambiguous as soon as we cut more than 3.0.0-ONAP off of it

Patch Set 1:

James is handling the APPC change

Patch Set 1:

(2 comments)

Patch Set 1:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 1:

> (2 comments)
 > 
 > I always had an issue
 > with the fact that the manifest is only casablanca not 3.0.0-ONAP

Not sure if this is what you were referring to, but the integration repo (and thereby the manifests) does have a 3.0.0-ONAP tag which represents the official set of docker image versions for the initial Casablanca release.  We will likewise tag the set of docker image versions for Casablanca MR as 3.0.1-ONAP.
